danger_signals.visual_form: Player sprite placement on field relative to ball carrier
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity of defenders indicates imminent tackle threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Goalkeeper animation stance and positioning in goal box
danger_signals.meaning: Shot accuracy is threatened; danger to scoring opportunity

danger_signals.visual_form: Ball transfers from player control to opponent team
danger_signals.meaning: Shift from offensive to defensive world-state; immediate counter-attack risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Vacant grid areas between player and goal, minimal opponent sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passing lane or clear scoring opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Allied player sprites positioned near ball carrier
safety_signals.meaning: Available pass targets reduce interception risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Goalkeeper sprite far from ball in defensive alignment
safety_signals.meaning: Safe possession; low immediate shot threat

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary jersey color (varies by real-world team affiliation)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team crest/logo displayed on jersey and in UI
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Away team uses contrasting jersey colors to distinguish allegiance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Home team bench and substitutes positioned on designated sideline; home crowd audio cues

faction_indicators.color_identity: Secondary/alternate jersey color (white or contrasting to home)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinct from home team through color differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Away team bench on opposite sideline; audio commentary distinguishes away team allegiance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All 22 player sprites visible on field; ball sprite active; scoreboard displays current time/score
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players move in response to ball position and tactical AI; continuous animation cycles for running, passing, shooting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprites freeze in formation; ball positioned at designated set piece location; UI may highlight available actions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Animation paused pending player input; AI determines formation and player positioning for defensive wall or set piece execution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ball enters goal frame; scoreboard increments; celebration animations triggered; possible crowd reaction audio
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scoring team players trigger celebration animations; match temporarily halts; ball reset to center field

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Referee sprite animation or visual feedback; ball stops; yellow/red card UI may display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player animation freezes at foul location; set piece awarded to opposing team

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Field cleared of players; scoreboard shows final/interim score; UI displays match statistics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All player animations cease; transition to menu or next match state

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of 3000+ real players has documented position, transfer history, and ranking visible in roster—communicates competitive hierarchy and team composition authenticity
Inclusion of authentic leagues and teams with correct crests and colors tells player they are simulating actual professional soccer ecosystem
Available viewing perspectives (standard, side, overhead, tactical) allow players to observe tactical field geography; different angles reveal different information about positioning and spacing
Crowd noise volume and intensity varies with match momentum, goal scoring, and crowd proximity to action—communicates emotional stakes and home field advantage
Audio commentary describes player actions, names, and match events, reinforcing real-world player identity and tactical narrative
UI systems for roster management visually represent squad composition changes, communicating squad depth and strategic player allocation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite animates forward sprint; can execute passing, shooting, or dribbling actions; movement speed increases when sprinting
Sprite orients toward ball carrier; moves to intercept or shadow; AI attempts to close distance and force turnover
Stands in neutral ready position; positioned in goal box
Animation responds to incoming ball; extends limbs to block/catch; movement becomes reactive and dynamic
Repositions between defense and attack; available as pass target; behavior shifts based on ball location and match phase
Maintains proximity to assigned defender; restricts movement and passing lanes; animation shows pressuring stance

notes: FIFA Soccer 96 is a sports simulation with limited narrative world-state complexity compared to action/adventure paradigms. Manifestation systems focus on: (1) Real-world team/player identity as visual-behavioral anchors (jersey colors, names, crests); (2) Match state transitions (active play → set piece → goal → halftime) communicated through sprite freezing, scoreboard updates, and animation cycles; (3) Tactical positioning as danger/safety cue (defensive clustering = danger; open field = safety); (4) Audio-visual synergy (crowd, commentary) reinforces authentic match narrative. The 'world-state' is primarily match progress and ball possession, not political/faction conflict. Visual communication is deterministic rather than emergent—sprites reflect AI-driven tactical formation rather than dynamic faction interaction.
